ddls:
CREATE TABLE "events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event */,
  "event_date" TEXT /* Date of the event */,
  "event_time" TEXT /* Time of the event */,
  "event_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the event */,
  "chapter_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the chapter or affiliate hosting the event */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the event (e.g., in-person, virtual) */,
  "event_type_id" INTEGER /* Type of the event (e.g., in-person, virtual) */,
  "competency_id" INTEGER /* Primary competency of the event */,
  "registration_start_date" TEXT /* Date when registration opens */,
  "registration_end_date" TEXT /* Date when registration closes */,
  "maximum_capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum number of attendees allowed */,
  "current_attendee_count" INTEGER /* Current number of registered attendees */,
  "event_status" TEXT /* Status of the event (e.g., planned, ongoing, completed, canceled) */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the event */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the event was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User who last updated the event */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date the event was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_event_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_type_id") REFERENCES event_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_competency_id FOREIGN KEY ("competency_id") REFERENCES competencies ("competency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_chapter_id FOREIGN KEY ("chapter_id") REFERENCES chapters ("chapter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "chapters" (
  "chapter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each chapter or affiliate */,
  "chapter_name" TEXT /* Name of the chapter or affiliate */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the chapter or affiliate */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the chapter or affiliate */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the chapter */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the chapter was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User who last updated the chapter */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date the chapter was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("chapter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the event type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "competencies" (
  "competency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each competency */,
  "competency_name" TEXT /* Name of the competency */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the competency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("competency_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_attendees" (
  "attendee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attendee */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event being attended */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user attending the event */,
  "attendance_status" TEXT /* Status of attendance (e.g., registered, attended) */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date the user registered for the event */,
  "payment_status" TEXT /* Payment status (e.g., unpaid, paid) */,
  "cancellation_date" TEXT /* Date the user canceled their registration */,
  "cancellation_reason" TEXT /* Reason for cancellation */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the registration */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the registration was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User who last updated the registration */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date the registration was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attendee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_attendees_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_attendees_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, chapter leader, attendee) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* File path to the user's profile picture */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Brief description of the user */,
  "social_media_links" TEXT /* Links to the user's social media profiles */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the user account */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User who last updated the user account */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_materials" (
  "material_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event material */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event the material belongs to */,
  "material_name" TEXT /* Name of the material */,
  "material_file" TEXT /* File path to the material */,
  "material_type" TEXT /* Type of the material (e.g., presentation, handout) */,
  "material_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the material */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the material */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the material was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User who last updated the material */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date the material was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("material_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_materials_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "messages" (
  "message_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each message */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user sending the message */,
  "recipient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the message */,
  "message_text" TEXT /* Text of the message */,
  "send_date" TEXT /* Date the message was sent */,
  "read_status" TEXT /* Read status of the message (e.g., unread, read) */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the message */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the message was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User who last updated the message */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date the message was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("message_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_messages_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_messages_recipient_id FOREIGN KEY ("recipient_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
